ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and rolls — white wheat bread

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium white bread roll weighing approximately 70 g, suitable as a single serving for a sandwich or as a side bread at a meal.

nutrient_source.title: White bread roll — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for white bread roll (cr_id F05030030). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread roll analogous entry; confirmed by University of Sydney GI Database white bread category (GI ~70–75)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database range for white wheat bread rolls (GI 70–75, glucose reference). Glycemic load per 70 g serving calculated as (75 × 51.6 × 0.70) / 100 = 27.1. This is classified as high GL (≥20).

hypertension.guidance: A single 70 g white bread roll delivers approximately 396 mg sodium — nearly 20% of the 2000 mg daily limit — making regular consumption a meaningful contributor to elevated blood pressure in sodium-sensitive individuals; lower-sodium wholegrain alternatives are strongly preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and high carbohydrate load (51.6 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (3.5 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: White bread rolls cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation due to their high GI and refined flour base; people with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size, choose wholegrain rolls with fibre >5 g/100 g, and pair with protein or healthy fat to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (566 mg/100 g) and refined carbohydrate content contributing to dyslipidaemia and blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of white bread rolls contributes to elevated sodium intake and refined carbohydrate load, both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk; substituting with wholegrain rolls reduces sodium exposure and improves the dietary fibre profile relevant to LDL cholesterol management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A white bread roll is a small, individually portioned baked bread product made primarily from refined white wheat flour, water, yeast, salt, sugar, and a small amount of fat (oil or butter). The roll is typically round or oval, with a soft, slightly chewy crumb and a lightly golden crust produced by oven baking at high temperature. It is a staple bread product widely consumed across Singapore, Southeast Asia, and globally, commonly served as a sandwich carrier, a side bread at meals, or as a breakfast item. Nutritionally, a white bread roll provides 290 kcal per 100 g, with 10.2 g protein, 3.8 g fat, and 51.6 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notably high at 566 mg/100 g, and the dietary fibre is modest at 3.5 g/100 g. The glycemic index is high (~75), meaning it causes rapid blood glucose elevation, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or insulin resistance. The high sodium content is a concern for hypertension management. From a cardiovascular perspective, the refined carbohydrate and sodium combination warrants moderation. Wholegrain alternatives with higher fibre and lower sodium are recommended for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a white bread roll?
Is white bread roll suitable for diabetics?
White bread roll GI glycemic index
Can I eat bread rolls with high blood pressure?
White roll vs wholegrain roll nutrition

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030030) at confidence 0.95. GI value of 75 is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database range for white wheat bread rolls. Saturated fat and detailed fatty acid breakdown are not reported in the HPB source and are set to -1 accordingly.
notes: saturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed saturated fat breakdown not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this item. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: fatty acid fractions not available in the HPB source. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry as of 2026-04-16. The input dietary_tags field contained 'high_fibre'; this tag has been removed as 3.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g does not meet the Singapore HPB high-fibre threshold of ≥5 g/100 g. GI glycemic load per serving calculated for a 70 g standard serving: (75 × 51.6 × 0.70) / 100 = 27.1 g GL (high). This food is not a Singaporean dish by origin; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null. White bread roll is a generic Western staple; G6 multilingual rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply.

improvement_actions_taken: Removed unsupported 'high_fibre' dietary tag (3.5 g fibre/100 g does not meet HPB ≥5 g/100 g threshold).
Corrected health condition key from 'diabetes' to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
Added cardiovascular_disease health context (missing from input).
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with source justification.
Calculated glycemic load per 70 g serving (27.1) from GI and carbohydrate content.
Populated full ontology section with defensible FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes.
Expanded aliases to 7 English variants with consistent aliases_by_language structure.
Added full llm_training section with natural_language_description >200 characters.
Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance: white bread roll is a generic Western staple, not a Singaporean dish; local-language fields correctly set to null.
